This allows users to claim their funds and immediately use them in other operations like DEX trades, all in a single transaction.","errors":{"CallFailed(uint256)":[{"details":"Thrown when one of the arbitrary calls fails","params":{"index":"The index of the failed call in the calls array"}}],"ClaimInvalidAddress()":[{"details":"Thrown when the claimer address doesn't match the transaction sender"}],"InsufficientBalance()":[{"details":"Thrown when the router's balance is below the required minimum for the operation"}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}],"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}],"SwapCallNotAllowed()":[{"details":"Thrown when a call targets a swap contract"}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"claimCall((bytes32,uint256,address,uint256,uint8,bytes32,bytes32),address,bytes)":{"details":"Claims funds from the EtherSwap contract and performs a single external call forwarding the claimed Ether","params":{"callData":"The encoded function calldata for the call","callee":"The contract address to call after claiming","claim":"The claim parameters for the EtherSwap contract"}},"claimCall((bytes32,uint256,address,uint256,uint8,bytes32,bytes32),address,bytes,uint8,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Claims funds from the EtherSwap contract and performs a single external call, authorized via EIP-712 signature","params":{"callData":"The encoded function calldata for the call","callee":"The contract address to call after claiming","claim":"The claim parameters for the EtherSwap contract","r":"First 32 bytes of the EIP-712 signature authorizing this call","s":"Second 32 bytes of the EIP-712 signature authorizing this call","v":"Recovery identifier (27 or 28); The router derives the native fee from its post-call Ether balance.","calls":"Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming","claim":"The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract","oft":"The OFT contract that executes the cross-chain send","sendData":"The destination and message options for the OFT send. claimCall((bytes32,uint256,address,uint2.details: Claims funds from the EtherSwap contract and performs a single external call forwarding the claimed Ether

params.callData: The encoded function calldata for the call
params.callee: The contract address to call after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the EtherSwap contract

claimCall((bytes32,uint256,address,uint2.details: Claims funds from the EtherSwap contract and performs a single external call, authorized via EIP-712 signature

params.callData: The encoded function calldata for the call
params.callee: The contract address to call after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the EtherSwap contract
params.r: First 32 bytes of the EIP-712 signature authorizing this call
params.s: Second 32 bytes of the EIP-712 signature authorizing this call
params.v: Recovery identifier (27 or 28); the final byte of a packed 65-byte EIP-712 signature authorizing this call

claimERC20Call((bytes32,uint256,address,.details: Claims tokens from the ERC20Swap contract and performs a single external call

params.callData: The encoded function calldata for the call
params.callee: The contract address to call after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract

claimERC20Call((bytes32,uint256,address,.details: Claims tokens from the ERC20Swap contract and performs a single external call, authorized via EIP-712 signature

params.callData: The encoded function calldata for the call
params.callee: The contract address to call after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract
params.r: First 32 bytes of the EIP-712 signature authorizing this call
params.s: Second 32 bytes of the EIP-712 signature authorizing this call
params.v: Recovery identifier (27 or 28); the final byte of a packed 65-byte EIP-712 signature authorizing this call

claimERC20Execute((bytes32,uint256,addre.details: Claims tokens from the ERC20Swap contract, executes arbitrary calls, then sweeps remaining funds

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract
params.minAmountOut: The minimum amount to sweep to the claimer
params.token: The token address to sweep (address(0) for Ether)

claimERC20Execute((bytes32,uint256,addre.details: Claims tokens from the ERC20Swap contract, executes arbitrary calls, then sweeps remaining funds to a specified destination This version uses EIP-712 signature verification to allow the claimer to authorize someone else to execute the claim

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract
params.destination: The address where the swept funds will be sent
params.minAmountOut: The minimum amount to sweep to the destination
params.token: The token address to sweep (address(0) for Ether)
params.v: Recovery identifier (27 or 28); the final byte of a packed 65-byte signature

claimERC20ExecuteCctp((bytes32,uint256,a.details: Claims tokens from the ERC20Swap contract, executes arbitrary calls, then burns the router's balance through CCTP This version uses EIP-712 signature verification to allow the claimer to authorize someone else to execute the claim

params.auth: The signed authorization for the CCTP burn, including the minimum remaining amount
params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming
params.cctpData: The destination and settlement options for the CCTP burn
params.claim: The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract
params.token: The token whose router balance will be burned
params.tokenMessenger: The CCTP Token Messenger contract that executes the burn

claimERC20ExecuteOft((bytes32,uint256,ad.details: Claims tokens from the ERC20Swap contract, executes arbitrary calls, then sends the router's balance through an OFT token This version uses EIP-712 signature verification to allow the claimer to authorize someone else to execute the claim

params.auth: The signed authorization for the OFT send, including `minAmountLd`, `lzTokenFee`, and the refund address. The router derives the native fee from its post-call Ether balance.
params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the ERC20Swap contract
params.oft: The OFT contract that executes the cross-chain send
params.sendData: The destination and message options for the OFT send. The router derives both `amountLD` and `minAmountLD` from the post-call token balance.
params.token: The token whose router balance will be sent

claimExecute((bytes32,uint256,address,ui.details: Claims funds from the swap contract, executes arbitrary calls, then sweeps remaining funds

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the EtherSwap contract
params.minAmountOut: The minimum amount to sweep to the claimer
params.token: The token address to sweep (address(0) for Ether)

claimExecute((bytes32,uint256,address,ui.details: Claims funds from the swap contract, executes arbitrary calls, then sweeps remaining funds to a specified destination This version uses EIP-712 signature verification to allow the claimer to authorize someone else to execute the claim

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute after claiming
params.claim: The claim parameters for the EtherSwap contract
params.destination: The address where the swept funds will be sent
params.minAmountOut: The minimum amount to sweep to the destination
params.token: The token address to sweep (address(0) for Ether)
params.v: Recovery identifier (27 or 28); the final byte of a packed 65-byte signature

constructor.details: Constructor sets the EtherSwap and ERC20Swap contract addresses

params.erc20SwapContract: The address of the ERC20Swap contract to interact with
params.permit2Contract: The address of the Permit2 contract to interact with
params.swapContract: The address of the EtherSwap contract to interact with

executeAndLock(bytes32,address,address,u.details: Executes arbitrary calls, then locks the remaining Ether balance in the swap contract

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute before locking
params.claimAddress: The address that can claim the locked Ether
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash for the swap
params.refundAddress: The address that can refund the locked Ether
params.timelock: The block height after which a refund becomes possible

executeAndLockERC20(bytes32,address,addr.details: Executes arbitrary calls, then locks the remaining token balance in the swap contract

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute before locking
params.claimAddress: The address that can claim the locked tokens
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash for the swap
params.refundAddress: The address that can refund the locked tokens
params.timelock: The block height after which a refund becomes possible
params.tokenAddress: The ERC20 token address to lock

executeAndLockERC20WithPermit2(bytes32,a.details: Executes arbitrary calls, transfers tokens via Permit2, then locks the remaining token balance

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute before locking
params.claimAddress: The address that can claim the locked tokens
params.owner: The address of the token owner who signed the permit
params.permit: Permit2 transfer permit signed by the token owner
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash for the swap
params.refundAddress: The address that can refund the locked tokens
params.signature: Signature over the Permit2 transfer permit and witness data
params.timelock: The block height after which a refund becomes possible
params.tokenAddress: The ERC20 token address to lock

executeCctp((address,uint256,bytes)[],ad.details: Executes arbitrary calls, then burns the router's token balance through CCTP

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute before the CCTP burn
params.cctpData: The destination and settlement options for the CCTP burn
params.minAmount: The minimum amount that must remain for the CCTP burn after executing `calls`
params.token: The token whose router balance will be burned
params.tokenMessenger: The CCTP Token Messenger contract that executes the burn

executeOft((address,uint256,bytes)[],add.details: Executes arbitrary calls, then sends the router's token balance through an OFT token

params.calls: Array of arbitrary calls to execute before the OFT send
params.lzTokenFee: The LayerZero token fee to use for the send
params.minAmountLd: The minimum amount that must remain for the OFT send after executing `calls`
params.oft: The OFT contract that executes the cross-chain send
params.refundAddress: The address that receives any excess native fee refund from the OFT
params.sendData: The destination and message options for the OFT send
params.token: The token whose router balance will be sent

claimERC20Call((bytes32,uint256,address,.notice: This function does not sweep remaining funds to the claimer, so ensure that the callee consumes all tokens
This function does not sweep remaining funds to the claimer, so ensure that the callee consumes all tokens
